issues.current_text: the element at index `i`, or throws `IndexOutOfBoundsException` if `i` is out of bounds
issues.problem: The `@return` tag describes throwing behavior, but exceptions are not return values. The method already has a `@throws[IndexOutOfBoundsException]` annotation that documents the exception path. Mixing throw semantics into `@return` is non-idiomatic Scaladoc and duplicates information that belongs in `@throws`.

general_feedback: Only one tag was filled in (the `@return` on `apply` at line 89). The added text is factually accurate but combines two distinct concerns — the returned value and the exceptional path — into the `@return` tag. Since `@throws[IndexOutOfBoundsException]` is already present on the method, the `@return` should describe only the value returned on success. Keeping it to a single concise clause also matches the surrounding Scaladoc style in this file (e.g., `@return` entries for `indexOf`, `lastIndexOf`, etc., describe only the return value, with exceptional paths handled separately).
